context: The agent processes a discovery.json with verdict=AMBIGUOUS. This scenario tests whether the agent correctly branches on the AMBIGUOUS verdict: surfaces the sub_brands_detected list, displays the guidance field, and emits the required re-invoke message — then stops without attempting to present candidates or write a selection.json.

checklist.name: All sub-brands listed
checklist.description: selection-output.md lists all three detected sub-brands: 'DataBridge Cloud', 'DataBridge Edge', and 'DataBridge Streams'.

checklist.name: Guidance field surfaced
checklist.description: selection-output.md includes the text of the guidance field from discovery.json (or a close paraphrase) — the explanation of why the company is ambiguous and what the sub-brand surfaces are.

checklist.name: Re-invoke message present
checklist.description: selection-output.md contains a message instructing the caller to re-invoke the discovery skill with a 'parent/sub-brand' format (e.g. 'databridge/databridge-cloud').

checklist.name: AMBIGUOUS verdict stated
checklist.description: selection-output.md explicitly states the verdict is 'AMBIGUOUS' (or 'Ambiguous') — the verdict value from the discovery file is surfaced, not silently implied.

checklist.name: No selection.json created
checklist.description: No file named selection.json exists anywhere in the workspace — the workflow correctly terminates after the AMBIGUOUS branch without persisting a selection.

checklist.name: No candidates presented
checklist.description: selection-output.md does NOT contain a ranked candidate table or numbered list of skill_targets — Step 2 (Sort Candidates) was not attempted.

checklist.name: Workflow stops early
checklist.description: selection-output.md does not ask the user to pick a target or prompt for a selection — the workflow finished at the AMBIGUOUS branch.

checklist.name: Sub-brands shown individually
checklist.description: selection-output.md lists each sub-brand as a separate item (not collapsed into a single sentence) — the sub_brands_detected array is expanded into individual entries.
